tldr: About a dozen of your live ads are running the same wording, and roughly half have been up for 70+ days. Meta sees these look-alike ads as basically one ad, so it quietly picks one and stops showing the rest - you paid to make ads that almost no one sees.

benchmark.context: The typical brand your size ships fresh ads every 3 weeks. The best brands in your space are on a 2-week rhythm. Several of your ads are past 75 days, which is well past the point where they stop pulling their weight.

ads.headline: No Fake Sugars. Just Real Hydration
ads.body: Ditch the sugar crash without sacrificing flavor. Strive Soda delivers bold soda taste with 0 sugar, 0 calories, and real ingredients. Try all fan-favorite flavors in the Variety Pack - your new go-to refreshment.
drivers: Copy-paste twin of another swipeable ad you already have running, just pointed at a different page.
89 days live, so the people most likely to buy have already scrolled past it many times.
Two of your best selling points (honey and electrolytes) never appear in this ad's body.

ads.headline: Zero Sugar. Full Flavor.
ads.body: Ditch the sugar crash without sacrificing flavor. Strive Soda delivers bold soda taste with 0 sugar, 0 calories, and real ingredients. Try all fan-favorite flavors in the Variety Pack - your new go-to refreshment.
drivers: Just a new headline slapped on the same body copy you're running under three other headlines.
89 days of runtime with a first-sip claim (0 calories) that another live ad of yours contradicts (45 calories).
That contradiction across your own ads makes the whole account feel less trustworthy to a shopper.

ads.headline: WHY EVERYONE IS REACHING FOR STRIVE RIGHT NOW
ads.body: Some drinks feel refreshing at first… then leave you feeling heavy. Strive was made to feel different. Sweetened with honey and infused with electrolytes for hydration, it delivers crisp carbonation and balanced flavor in a lighter soda you can actually enjoy. Refreshing, easy to drink, and designed for active days. That's why more people are reaching for Strive.
drivers: Three separate live ads use this exact headline and body, one static and two more static images.
The all-caps headline shouts but doesn't say anything a shopper can picture or remember.
76 days in, and there's no offer, price, or reason to click today instead of next week.

ads.headline: WHY EVERYONE IS REACHING FOR STRIVE RIGHT NOW
ads.body: Some drinks feel refreshing at first… then leave you feeling heavy. Strive was made to feel different. Sweetened with honey and infused with electrolytes for hydration, it delivers crisp carbonation and balanced flavor in a lighter soda you can actually enjoy. Refreshing, easy to drink, and designed for active days. That's why more people are reaching for Strive.
drivers: A direct duplicate of another live ad, same words, same image style, same button.
You are paying to make two versions of one ad, and Meta shows only one of them.
76 days old with no fresh angle, so the same tired people keep seeing the same tired frame.

ads.headline: The Soda That Gets Me Through the Day
ads.body: Hydration becomes simpler when the drink matches real habits. It works better when the drink feels easy to enjoy. When water feels dull and soda feels like too much, Strive bridges that gap. Light fizz, hydration support, and a variety pack with two delicious flavors: Peach Perfect and Tropical Recharge.
drivers: The opening lines read like filler and never say anything a shopper hasn't heard before.
88 days live, so anyone who was going to click has already had many chances.
You have a video with this exact same script running too, and only one of the two is actually being served.

ads_compact.headline: Get 15% OFF now
ads_compact.body: Win $100+ Worth of Strive Soda + Get 15% OFF Today. Here's how it works: enter your email, get 15% off instantly, then place your first order to automatically enter the quarterly drawing.

ads_compact.headline: Subscribe & save 30%
ads_compact.body: Save 30% today when you subscribe! Kids love soda. Parents hate the sugar that comes with it. That's why we created Strive - a better-for-you soda with crisp taste, naturally sweetened with organic wildflower honey, and packed with electrolytes for hydration. Finally, a soda kids enjoy and parents actually feel good about serving.

ads_compact.headline: Daily Soda, Upgraded With Function
ads_compact.body: Soda is part of your daily routine. Now it can do more than just taste good. Strive is a better-for-you hydration soda made with 180mg essential electrolytes, 100% organic wildflower honey, and only 45 calories per can.

hero_concept.hook: The soda you don't have to feel guilty about.
hero_concept.headline: Peach, poured over ice.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Most sodas leave you sticky, sluggish, and thirsty ten minutes later. Strive Peach is built the other way around.

100% organic wildflower honey, 180mg of electrolytes, 45 calories a can, and a peach flavor that actually tastes like peach - not candy. One 12-pack, one flavor, no guessing.

Grab your 12-pack of Peach today. 20% off your first order with code HONEY20.
hero_concept.visual_direction: A single chilled Strive Peach can sitting on wet stone next to a glass of soda over ice with fresh peach slices, condensation beading on the can, warm afternoon light coming from the side.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Almost every live ad you have points to the Variety Pack with the same 'zero sugar' opening line. Nothing sells one flavor at a time, and nothing shows the drink actually being poured and enjoyed, so shoppers never get hungry for a specific can.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a summertime kitchen counter scene with a chilled Strive Peach can sitting on wet dark stone next to a clear glass filled with peach-colored soda over ice cubes and two fresh peach slices, with warm golden late-afternoon side light streaming in from the left creating soft highlights on the can and glistening condensation droplets across the aluminum. Clean editorial beverage photography aesthetic with shallow depth of field, natural warm tones, and a slightly out-of-focus warm neutral background. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL - preserve the exact Strive Soda Peach can colorway, artwork, and finish as shown in the reference image: the specific peach/orange brand color of the can body, the Strive logo, the flavor name 'Peach', and every printed graphic, illustration, and piece of text on the can exactly as printed - strictly NO color shift toward red, yellow, or pink, NO changing the peach tone, NO altering or removing any printed brand text, logo, flavor name, nutrition callouts, or graphics on the can. STRUCTURE - preserve the exact construction: a standard 12oz slim aluminum soda can with a single pull-tab top, the Strive Peach printed wrap graphic running the full body of the can with the brand logo, flavor name 'Peach', and all secondary text and illustrations in the exact positions and colors shown in the reference. PRESERVE every printed element on the can wrap exactly - all logos, all text, all graphics, all colors, same placement. Do NOT add extra cans unless as a single glass companion, do NOT change the can shape or size, do NOT alter the pull-tab, do NOT remove or modify any printed brand text, logo, flavor name, or graphic on the can wrap, do NOT swap the peach color palette for a different flavor's palette. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself - text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Your six oldest 'WHY EVERYONE IS REACHING' and 'No Fake Sugars' ads stop pulling their weight inside the next two weeks.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Strive Soda refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Christopher, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads - the pictures, the words, and how long each one has been running. No access to your account or private numbers.
